issue: c1 contains '90 mg' and c2 is '10%'; the dose calculation (10% of 90 mg) directly links them
c1 is '7–8 weeks' and c2 is 'before 4 weeks'; the boundary of 4 weeks is explicitly compared to the 7–8 week target
c1 range '0.9–1.5' directly precedes c2 '0.3 per 1,000'; this creates an implicit contrast that links the two numbers
Overlapping numeric ranges for two cloze answers: c1='1 year' vs c2='1–3 years' creates ambiguity on whether 1-year interval applies to both tests or just one.
c1 'against' is repeated in the surrounding text ('recommends {{c1::against}}'). The cloze answer 'against' appears verbatim in the visible template.
Numeric boundary leak: c1 requires K⁺ >5.5 to withhold; c2 shows reinitiation at K⁺ ≤5.0. When c1 is hidden, the visible '≤5.0' reveals the withholding threshold is >5.0 and likely 5.5
Paired single-fact split: c1 and c2 represent two constraints on the SAME decision (cumulative dose cap vs fertility-preservation cap). Knowing one significantly constrains the other
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is >12 hours/day, c2 shows <150 mmHg; these are independent thresholds for different measurements (time vs oxygen ratio) with no logical spoiler relationship

Paired single-fact split: c1 and c2 both specify the same risk stratification system components (three-strata at diagnosis vs four-strata at follow-up), but they are independent time-point definitions, not a spoiler
Numeric boundary leak: c1 range 4–5 and c2 range 6–8 are adjacent/mutually exclusive thresholds; knowing one excludes the other
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is <30×10⁹/L; c2 (corticosteroids) is the answer. The threshold value <30 is part of the eligibility criterion and doesn't directly spoil the treatment choice
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is ≥2 and c2 is ≥3; these are adjacent thresholds where knowing one defines the boundary for the other

Paired single fact: c1 and c2 are old vs new ferritin threshold cutoffs for the same diagnosis (iron deficiency); they are two halves of one guideline update and learning one directly reveals the other

Numeric boundary leak: c1 is 30 mL/min cutoff for LMWH/fondaparinux; c2 is 15 mL/min for apixaban/edoxaban. When c1 is hidden on card 2, the cutoff <30 is visible, which constrains the c2 answer to a lower threshold
Mutually-exclusive list members: c1, c2, c3 are three distinct time-based response definitions (1 week, 1 month, 6 months). The temporal ordering makes elimination trivial
Numeric boundary leak: c2 is <5 mg/day; when c2 is hidden, readers see '<5 mg/day' and trivially infer the threshold
Numeric boundary leak: c2 contains '≤50 mg/day in CKD stage ≥3' while c1 is '≤100 mg/day'; when c1 is hidden, the boundary 50 is visible in c2's visible body
Literal text repetition and list elimination: c2 explicitly names 'IL-17i' and 'etanercept', which are the two agents being contrasted in c1; hiding c1 leaves the exclusion criteria visible
Paired single-fact split: c1 and c2 are two sequential steps of one inseparable DMAR tapering rule; they define a single clinical action
Literal text repetition: c2's dose values (1.2 mg, 0.6 mg, 1 hour) directly instantiate and leak the answer to c1 ('low-dose'); when c1 is hidden, the visible text shows the exact low-dose regimen

Paired single-fact split: c1 (270 doses) and c2 (76 doses) are the two halves of one inseparable rule about the 9H regimen variants — disclosing one directly implies the other via simple arithmetic/context.

Numeric boundary leak: c1 states 25 mg/kg (children 2–11), c2 states 15 mg/kg (adults) — showing c2's card reveals the adult dose, leaving only the child dose as the hidden answer, making the distinction leak.
Grammar tell: 'an experienced' before consonant 'e' in 'experienced' is correct, but the cloze reveals the answer will start with a vowel sound.

Numeric boundary leak: c1 threshold '<0.1 mU/L' combined with c2 '≥65 years' leaves visible '≥65' which doesn't directly spoil the TSH cutoff, but the relationship between TSH suppression and age is the core distinction.
Paired single-fact split: BMI thresholds (≥30 and ≥27 with comorbidity) are two conditions of one rule for pharmacotherapy eligibility.

Literal repetition: c1 text contains 'MMI' and 'PTU' but the cloze is for 'MMI', and the visible text later says 'PTU' for thyroid storm/first trimester, creating ambiguity about which agent is preferred.
numeric_boundary leak: c1 upper bound (8 mEq/L) overlaps with c2/c3 lower bounds (10–12 and 8 mEq/L); hiding c1 leaves visible c2/c3 thresholds that constrain the answer

numeric_boundary leak: c1 gives LSM range (20–25 kPa) which, when hidden, still leaves visible reference to 'platelets < 150 K/mm³'; the range itself appears in c1 and again in c2 (15–20 kPa), creating redundant threshold exposure
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is 2–4 weeks, c2 is ≥30 days; hiding c1 leaves visible '≥30 days' which bounds c1's range trivially
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is <60, c2 is <187; hiding c1 leaves '<187' visible, which leaks that c1 is a lower threshold
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is ≥6 weeks, c2 is ≤25 mL/min; these are alternative criteria and together form a paired single-fact rule (either condition triggers indication)
Numeric boundary leak: c1 is ≥1000 ng/mL, c2 is <500 ng/mL; hiding c1 leaves visible '<500' which leaks the threshold comparison

numeric_boundary: c1 specifies 130–<140 mm Hg, but the visible text then states 'stop antihypertensives if SBP falls below {{c1::130 mm Hg}}', where c1 is repeated with the same value. This is self-consistent but the stopping rule at 130 is directly adjacent to the target range's lower bound.
numeric_boundary: c1 target is 0.5–0.9 ng/mL; c2 threshold for toxicity is ≥1.2 ng/mL. Hiding c1 leaves the toxicity threshold visible, allowing derivation of the safe upper bound.
Numeric boundary leak: c1 (≤55%) and c2 (>50 mm) are paired thresholds for the same decision (AVR indication in severe AR); knowing one constrains the other.
Paired single fact: c1 (>180/120 mm Hg WITHOUT acute target organ damage) and c2 (do NOT use intermittent IV/oral) are two halves of one rule; removing c1 leaves the contraindication visible.

Numeric boundary leak: c1 (3 years for 3.0–3.9 cm), c2 (annually for 4.0–4.9 cm), and c3 (6 months for ≥5.0 cm) form a mutually-dependent threshold ladder; removing any one reveals the boundaries for others.
